title: Complexity Classes, Reductions, and Feasibility Boundaries · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Explain why a requested exact optimizer needs changed scope or a bounded alternative. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a feasibility and reduction decision memo with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate optimization questions to decision form without losing the threshold meaning.
Describe polynomial verification and solution concepts without claiming unresolved equalities.
Map instances and answers through a polynomial reduction in the correct hardness direction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
